evals.prompt: These files say the repository uses agents-stack, but the live tracked-work file is missing. Which child should own the next step?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/project-initializer because the repository cannot yet support honest sprint routing without durable live state.
files: AGENTS.md: This repository uses the agents-stack harness.
docs/live/tracked-work.json: missing

evals.prompt: The active sprint is already reconciled as review_failed with a clean restore boundary, but tracked work still has compound_pending_feature_ids for that same feature. Which child should run next?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/compound-capture because explicit compounding drains before any retry back into execution.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = WORKSTREAM-004; single_runnable_active_sprint = true; compound_pending_feature_ids = ["FEATURE-004"]; backlog entry FEATURE-004 has sprint_id WORKSTREAM-004 and status review_failed.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-004/review.md: FAIL verdict already recorded.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-004/status.json: phase = review_failed; clean_restore_ref = refs/worktrees/ws004-clean; attempt_count = 1; max_attempts = 3.

evals.prompt: A sprint is durably parked awaiting human input, there is no runnable active sprint, and the highest-priority dependency-ready backlog item is needs_brainstorm. Which child should run next?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/generator-brainstorm because parked work stays visible but does not occupy the one runnable slot when a dependency-ready brainstorm item exists.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = null; compound_pending_feature_ids = []; a parked sprint remains visible; backlog entry FEATURE-006 is highest priority, status needs_brainstorm, dependencies satisfied.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-005/status.json: phase = awaiting_human; resume_from = contract.md; human_action_required = update API credentials file.
docs/live/current-focus.md: current focus notes the parked blocker but names FEATURE-006 as the next dependency-ready backlog lane once routing resumes.

evals.prompt: No runnable sprint exists, no compound work is queued, and the highest-priority dependency-ready backlog item is pending rather than needs_brainstorm. Which child should run next?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/generator-proposal because the repository has proposal-ready backlog truth and nothing in compound, retry, or brainstorm lanes outranks it.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = null; compound_pending_feature_ids = []; backlog entry FEATURE-007 is highest priority, status pending, dependencies satisfied; no higher-priority needs_brainstorm item is dependency-ready.
docs/live/roadmap.md: FEATURE-007 is still an authorized remaining slice, not yet proposed.

evals.prompt: The active sprint already has a sprint proposal on disk, but there is still no approved contract. Which child should own the next step?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/evaluator-contract-review because the proposal exists and must be independently approved or rejected before implementation starts.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = WORKSTREAM-008; single_runnable_active_sprint = true; backlog entry FEATURE-008 has sprint_id WORKSTREAM-008 and status proposed.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-008/sprint_proposal.md: present.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-008/contract.md: missing.

evals.prompt: The sprint has an approved contract, no handoff yet, and no later-phase evidence outranks the contract. Which child should run next?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/generator-execution because implementation now owns the approved sprint contract.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = WORKSTREAM-009; single_runnable_active_sprint = true; backlog entry FEATURE-009 has sprint_id WORKSTREAM-009 and status contracted.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-009/contract.md: present with approved bounded slice.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-009/handoff.md: missing.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-009/status.json: phase = contracted.

evals.prompt: Execution produced a truthful handoff, build triage passed, and there is no review yet. Which child should run next?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/adversarial-live-review because the handoff exists and independent review now owns the next truthful checkpoint.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = WORKSTREAM-010; single_runnable_active_sprint = true; backlog entry FEATURE-010 has sprint_id WORKSTREAM-010 and status in_review.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-010/contract.md: present.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-010/runtime.md: present and build/startup checkpoint recorded.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-010/handoff.md: present.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-010/review.md: missing.

evals.prompt: A review already exists, but live and local state still tell an earlier-phase story. Which child should own the next step?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/state-update because review evidence exists and contradictory durable state must be reconciled before any new execution or proposal work.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: backlog entry FEATURE-011 still says pending and does not truthfully publish the review outcome.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-011/review.md: FAIL verdict recorded with required corrective actions.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-011/status.json: phase = contracted.

evals.prompt: The same sprint is marked awaiting_human locally but still advertised as the runnable active sprint globally. Which child should own the next step?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/state-update because active-versus-parked contradiction is a state integrity problem and the router must not invent a runnable winner.

evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/generator-execution because the failed review is already reconciled and the same sprint now qualifies for a clean retry.

evals.prompt: The sprint failed build/startup triage, but the failure is already reconciled, attempts remain, and a clean restore boundary is recorded. Which child should run next?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/generator-execution because build_failed retries belong to execution only when the durable retry guard can truthfully allow them.

evals.prompt: Tracked work still points at a failed sprint, but the retry metadata is unsafe or exhausted, so execution cannot truthfully restart yet. Which child should own the next step?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/state-update because retry routing must fail closed when clean restore metadata is missing or the attempt budget is exhausted.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = WORKSTREAM-015; single_runnable_active_sprint = true; compound_pending_feature_ids = []; backlog entry FEATURE-015 has sprint_id WORKSTREAM-015 and status build_failed.

evals.prompt: The only remaining non-terminal sprint is already durably parked, no dependency-ready backlog item can run around it, and no new human edits have changed the checkpoint. What should the router do?
evals.expected_output: No family child fits; answer directly because automation must wait on the durable human handoff boundary and there is no dependency-ready backlog work to route instead.

.harness/WORKSTREAM-016/status.json: phase = awaiting_human; resume_from = contract.md; human_action_required = human must edit the contract after stakeholder approval.
docs/live/current-focus.md: names WORKSTREAM-016 as parked and says automation is waiting on the human checkpoint.

evals.prompt: A prior session timed out while the sprint looked contracted in stale status text, but the strongest local artifact now shows a truthful handoff and no review. Which child should run next?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/adversarial-live-review because timeout resume still follows strongest durable artifact precedence, and a truthful handoff outranks stale execution resume hints.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = WORKSTREAM-017; single_runnable_active_sprint = true; backlog entry FEATURE-017 has sprint_id WORKSTREAM-017 and status contracted.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-017/contract.md: present.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-017/handoff.md: present with execution evidence and resume notes from before the timeout.
.harness/WORKSTREAM-017/status.json: phase = contracted; resume_from = contract.md; paused_reason = timeout.
docs/live/current-focus.md: stale timeout note still says to resume generator-execution from the contract checkpoint.

evals.prompt: A PASS review is already on disk, but the sprint has not yet truthfully published that outcome into local and live state. Which child should own the next step?
evals.expected_output: Routes to using-agents-stack/state-update because even a PASS review must reconcile through state-update before publish or archive logic can proceed.
files: docs/live/tracked-work.json: runnable_active_sprint_id = WORKSTREAM-018; single_runnable_active_sprint = true; backlog entry FEATURE-018 has sprint_id WORKSTREAM-018 and status in_review.
